gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A **cooperative** cancellation token (RFC-0008 §4.7; structured-concurrency cancellation, RT7)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Cancellation is **never preemptive**: a task observes the token at its own budget-check points (the"] # [doc = " same cadence it checks fuel/depth), so it can never be stopped mid-step in a way that drops an"] # [doc = " in-flight explicit outcome. Observing a cancelled token yields an explicit [`Cancelled`] — an"] # [doc = " *additive* outcome (RFC-0014 I1), never a silent termination. `Arc<AtomicBool>` so the token can be"] # [doc = " shared with tasks placed on other threads by the future runtime (M-357) without a redesign."] # [derive (Clone , Debug , Default)] pub struct CancelToken { flag : Arc < AtomicBool > , }

Idempotent; cooperative — it sets a flag the task observes at its next"] # [doc = " checkpoint, it does **not** interrupt a running step."] pub fn cancel (& self) { self . flag . store (true , Ordering :: SeqCst) ; } # [doc = " Whether cancellation has been requested."] # [must_use] pub fn is_cancelled (& self) -> bool { self . flag . load (Ordering :: SeqCst) } # [doc = " Observe the token at a checkpoint: an explicit [`Cancelled`] if cancellation was requested, else"] # [doc = " `Ok`. gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The **explicit, additive result of running a task** (RFC-0014 I1 lifted across the task boundary,"] # [doc = " RFC-0008 §4.7). gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A `reclaim` **supervisor** (RFC-0008 §4.7; RT4/RT7): it restarts a failed child under a *bounded*"] # [doc = " cascade, escalating (its own explicit failure) when the cascade hits **either** bound — the total"] # [doc = " `cascade` budget (M-353) **or** the windowed [`RestartIntensity`] rate. Both axes are honest and"] # [doc = " declared; neither lets a restart storm run away."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The logical clock is the supervisor's own monotonic counter ([`tick`](Supervisor::tick)); a driver"] # [doc = " advances it deterministically (one tick per supervised step, say). No wall clock is read (R8-Q3)."] # [derive (Debug , Clone)] pub struct Supervisor { intensity : RestartIntensity , budgets : Budgets , # [doc = " The logical tick of each restart still inside the current window (oldest at the front)."] restarts : VecDeque < u64 > , clock : u64 , }
gaps.reason: struct `Supervisor` uses named fields and references a type not resolvable in-file — emitting it positionally would introduce an unresolved reference that poisons the file's `myc check`; left gapped under the M-1006 resolvability gate (VR-5/G2)

gaps.snippet: impl Supervisor { # [doc = " A supervisor with a windowed [`RestartIntensity`] (the *rate* bound) and a total `cascade`"] # [doc = " budget of `max_total` restarts (the *cascade* bound). Both must hold for a restart to proceed."] # [must_use] pub fn new (intensity : RestartIntensity , max_total : u64) -> Self { Supervisor { intensity , budgets : Budgets :: new () . with (crate :: budget :: EffectBudget :: Depth (max_total)) , restarts : VecDeque :: new () , clock : 0 , } } # [doc = " The current logical tick."] # [must_use] pub fn now (& self) -> u64 { self . clock } # [doc = " Advance the logical clock by one tick and return the new value. A deterministic, monotonic"] # [doc = " counter — *not* wall-clock time (R8-Q3)."] pub fn tick (& mut self) -> u64 { self . clock += 1 ; self . clock } # [doc = " The total restart budget remaining (the `cascade` cap)."] # [must_use] pub fn restarts_remaining (& self) -> u64 { self . budgets . remaining (& EffectKind :: Cascade) . unwrap_or (0) } # [doc = " Record a restart at the current logical tick. Succeeds iff **both** bounds hold; otherwise the"] # [doc = " supervisor **escalates** with an explicit [`Escalation`] (never an unbounded storm)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Enforcement order (both are checked, both are honest): first the **total** `cascade` budget"] # [doc = " (M-353 channel) — a [`EffectBudgetExhausted`] becomes [`Escalation::CascadeBudgetExhausted`];"] # [doc = " then the **windowed** intensity *rate* — too many restarts within `window_ticks` becomes"] # [doc = " [`Escalation::IntensityExceeded`]."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = " Returns [`Escalation`] when the total cascade budget is exhausted or the windowed restart"] # [doc = " intensity is exceeded."] pub fn record_restart (& mut self) -> Result < () , Escalation > { self . budgets . consume (EffectKind :: Cascade , 1) . map_err (Escalation :: CascadeBudgetExhausted) ? ; let cutoff = self . clock . saturating_sub (self . intensity . window_ticks) ; while self . restarts . front () . is_some_and (| & t | t <= cutoff) { self . restarts . pop_front () ; } self . restarts . push_back (self . clock) ; let in_window = u32 :: try_from (self . restarts . len ()) . unwrap_or (u32 :: MAX) ; if in_window > self . intensity . max_restarts { return Err (Escalation :: IntensityExceeded { restarts_in_window : in_window , max_restarts : self . intensity . max_restarts , window_ticks : self . intensity . window_ticks , }) ; } Ok (()) } }

gaps.snippet: # [cfg (test)] mod tests { use super :: * ; # [test] fn cancellation_is_cooperative_and_explicit () { let tok = CancelToken :: new () ; assert ! (other . is_cancelled ()) ; } # [test] fn a_task_outcome_never_silently_succeeds_on_failure () { let done : TaskOutcome < u8 , & str > = TaskOutcome :: Done (1) ; assert ! (! done . is_failure ()) ; assert ! (TaskOutcome ::< u8 , & str >:: Failed ("boom") . is_failure ()) ; assert ! (TaskOutcome ::< u8 , & str >:: Cancelled . is_failure ()) ; assert ! (TaskOutcome ::< u8 , & str >:: BudgetExhausted (EffectBudgetExhausted { kind : EffectKind :: Retry , requested : 1 , remaining : 0 , }) . is_failure ()) ; } # [test] fn supervision_bounds_a_cascade_by_total_count () { let mut sup = Supervisor :: new (RestartIntensity { max_restarts : 100 , window_ticks : 1_000 , } , 2 ,) ; sup . tick () ; assert ! (sup . record_restart () . is_ok ()) ; assert ! (sup . record_restart () . is_ok ()) ; assert_eq ! (sup . restarts_remaining () , 0) ; match sup . record_restart () { Err (Escalation :: CascadeBudgetExhausted (e)) => assert_eq ! (e . kind , EffectKind :: Cascade) , other => panic ! ("expected a cascade-budget escalation, got {other:?}") , } } # [test] fn supervision_bounds_a_cascade_by_windowed_rate () { let mut sup = Supervisor :: new (RestartIntensity { max_restarts : 2 , window_ticks : 5 , } , 1_000 ,) ; sup . tick () ; assert ! (sup . record_restart () . is_ok ()) ; sup . tick () ; assert ! (sup . record_restart () . is_ok ()) ; sup . tick () ; match sup . record_restart () { Err (Escalation :: IntensityExceeded { restarts_in_window , max_restarts , .. }) => { assert_eq ! (restarts_in_window , 3) ; assert_eq ! (max_restarts , 2) ; } other => panic ! ("expected an intensity escalation, got {other:?}") , } for _ in 0 .. 10 { sup . tick () ; } assert ! (sup . record_restart () . is_ok () , "after the window clears, restarting is bounded-OK again") ; } # [test] fn cancelled_display_is_non_empty_and_descriptive () { let msg = Cancelled . to_string () ; assert ! (! msg . is_empty () , "Cancelled Display must not be empty (got empty string)") ; assert ! (msg . to_lowercase () . contains ("cancel") , "Cancelled Display must mention 'cancel'; got: {msg:?}") ; } # [test] fn escalation_display_is_non_empty_and_variant_specific () { let e = Escalation :: IntensityExceeded { restarts_in_window : 5 , max_restarts : 3 , window_ticks : 10 , } ; let msg = e . to_string () ; assert ! (! msg . is_empty () , "Escalation::IntensityExceeded Display must not be empty") ; assert ! (msg . contains ('5') || msg . contains ('3') , "IntensityExceeded Display must contain the restart counts; got: {msg:?}") ; let cascade_e = Escalation :: CascadeBudgetExhausted (EffectBudgetExhausted { kind : EffectKind :: Cascade , requested : 1 , remaining : 0 , }) ; let cascade_msg = cascade_e . to_string () ; assert ! (! cascade_msg . is_empty () , "Escalation::CascadeBudgetExhausted Display must not be empty") ; } # [test] fn supervisor_now_reflects_actual_tick_count () { let mut sup = Supervisor :: new (RestartIntensity { max_restarts : 100 , window_ticks : 1_000 , } , 1_000 ,) ; assert_eq ! (sup . now () , 0 , "initial tick is 0") ; sup . tick () ; assert_eq ! (sup . now () , 1 , "after 1 tick, now() must be 1 (not 0 or some constant)") ; sup . tick () ; assert_eq ! (sup . now () , 2 , "after 2 ticks, now() must be 2 (not 1 or 0)") ; for _ in 0 .. 8 { sup . tick () ; } assert_eq ! (sup . now () , 10 , "after 10 ticks total, now() must be 10") ; } # [test] fn supervisor_restarts_remaining_decrements_correctly () { let mut sup = Supervisor :: new (RestartIntensity { max_restarts : 100 , window_ticks : 1_000 , } , 5 ,) ; assert_eq ! (sup . restarts_remaining () , 5 , "before any restarts, remaining must be 5") ; sup . tick () ; sup . record_restart () . unwrap () ; assert_eq ! (sup . restarts_remaining () , 4 , "after 1 restart, remaining must be 4 (not 0)") ; sup . tick () ; sup . record_restart () . unwrap () ; assert_eq ! (sup . restarts_remaining () , 3 , "after 2 restarts, remaining must be 3 (not 0)") ; } }
